{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ōyasuba Kofun | native_name = 大塚山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =大安場古墳1号墳.JPG|image= File:Oyasuba Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōyasuba Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|37|21|23|N|140|24|12|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|37.355833|N|140.403611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Kōriyama, Fukushima|Kōriyama, Fukushima]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Kōriyama|WD=Q264206|1=Kōriyama}}| region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th, early 5th century AD; 0500| abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1996-1998 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōyasuba Kofun cluster'''|大安場古墳群|Ōyasuba Kofun gun}} is a group of five early to middle [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|megalithic tumuli]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Kōriyama, Fukushima|Kōriyama, Fukushima]] in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The largest of the tumuli at the site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/218189 |title= 大安場古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on the east side of the {{ill|Abukuma River|en|Abukuma River|WD=Q335853|lt=Abukuma River|1=Abukuma River}}, which flows through from the south to the north, at an altitude of about 250 meters. Discovered in 1991, the Koriyama City Board of Education conducted an excavation study from 1996 to 1998.
The largest tumulus is a {{nihongo|{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with the form of "two conjoined rectangles". It has a length of 83 meters and is orientated in a north-south direction. It was constructed by partly cutting out the natural hill next to the tumulus, with three tiers in the rear and two in the front. A large amount of red-colored pot-shaped {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} with a hole in the bottom was excavated from the slopes of the mound, and it was likely that these were originally kept on top of the mound. The interior has a clay-floored [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a length of 10 meters and width of two meters, containing a 9.2 meter long tree-coffin. The tomb had a large quantity of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, including swords, spears, sickles, fragments of armor as well as jewelry, which date the construction to around the second half of the 4th century
The remaining four {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular domed ''kofun'' have a diameter of approximately 15 meters each but were not well preserved. They are believed to date from the late 5th century and to be the tombs of the leaders of a nearby settlement.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], and is located a ten minute walk from the Kanaya bus stop on the Fukushima Kotsu bus from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kōriyama Station (Fukushima)|en|Kōriyama Station (Fukushima)|WD=Q385879|lt=Koriyama Station|1=Kōriyama Station (Fukushima)}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|WD=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}

{{ill|Kōriyama|WD=Q264206|1=Kōriyama}}| region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th, early 5th century AD; 0500| abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1996-1998 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōyasuba Kofun cluster'''|大安場古墳群|Ōyasuba Kofun gun}} is a group of five early to middle [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|megalithic tumuli]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Kōriyama, Fukushima|Kōriyama, Fukushima]] in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The largest of the tumuli at the site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/218189 |title= 大安場古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on the east side of the {{ill|Abukuma River|en|Abukuma River|WD=Q335853|lt=Abukuma River|1=Abukuma River}}, which flows through from the south to the north, at an altitude of about 250 meters. Discovered in 1991, the Koriyama City Board of Education conducted an excavation study from 1996 to 1998.
The largest tumulus is a {{nihongo|{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with the form of "two conjoined rectangles". It has a length of 83 meters and is orientated in a north-south direction. It was constructed by partly cutting out the natural hill next to the tumulus, with three tiers in the rear and two in the front. A large amount of red-colored pot-shaped {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} with a hole in the bottom was excavated from the slopes of the mound, and it was likely that these were originally kept on top of the mound. The interior has a clay-floored [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a length of 10 meters and width of two meters, containing a 9.2 meter long tree-coffin. The tomb had a large quantity of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, including swords, spears, sickles, fragments of armor as well as jewelry, which date the construction to around the second half of the 4th century
The remaining four {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular domed ''kofun'' have a diameter of approximately 15 meters each but were not well preserved. They are believed to date from the late 5th century and to be the tombs of the leaders of a nearby settlement.
The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], and is located a ten minute walk from the Kanaya bus stop on the Fukushima Kotsu bus from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kōriyama Station (Fukushima)|en|Kōriyama Station (Fukushima)|WD=Q385879|lt=Koriyama Station|1=Kōriyama Station (Fukushima)}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|WD=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}
